factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed trends in debt leverage. While it is actively deleveraging by reducing long-term debt and managing credit facilities effectively, debt leverage is also increasing due to new debt issuances and term loans to finance the Exelis acquisition.

evidence.text: Given our current cash position, outlook for funds generated from operations, credit ratings, available credit facility, cash needs and debt structure, we have not experienced, and do not expect to experience, any material issues with liquidity...

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends in debt leverage with both deleveraging and increased debt issuance.

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased compared to the previous year, primarily due to higher repayments of borrowings and cash dividends, as well as a decrease in net cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: The $161 million decrease in cash and cash equivalents from the end of fiscal 2015 to October 2, 2015 was primarily due to $173 million used for repayments of borrowings, which included $150 million of cash used to repay principal on our term loans...
Net cash provided by operating activities decreased 20.0 percent to $64 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with $80 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to higher repayments and reduced net cash from operations.
